mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate over the prior quarter signals easier discounting and lower financing stress, which raises S&P 500 prices over the next month. The signal is valid when the policy rate is actively declining and invalid when rates are flat or rising.
failure_condition: Reject if 20d SPX returns are not positive on average during quarters with a declining FEDFUNDS trend, or if the sign flips and negative returns dominate in easing windows across a rolling 3-year test.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in CPIAUCSL inflation above recent months raises expected policy restraint and discount rates, which compresses equity valuations and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect is strongest after an upside inflation surprise.
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-acceleration triggers are followed by positive 20d SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the triggered subset shows no negative mean return over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE indicates weakening household income and demand, which reduces expected sales and earnings revisions and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The signal is strongest when unemployment is increasing rather than simply high.
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-rise triggers do not precede negative 60d SPX returns on average, or if positive returns occur in more than half of triggered observations in a rolling 4-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth in RSAFS signals resilient consumer demand and supports near-term revenue expectations for large-cap firms, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect is falsified if sales growth is strong but equity returns are persistently weak.
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS momentum does not align with positive 20d SPX returns in at least 55% of observations, or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: An upward break in INDPRO with stable labor conditions signals stronger industrial activity and improving cyclical earnings, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism fails when industrial output rises but margin pressure from inflation offsets the benefit.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month change exceeds its prior 12-month median
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO breakout triggers are followed by negative or zero-average 20d SPX returns, or if the signal disappears once CPIAUCSL acceleration is controlled for.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in HOUST reflects weakening housing demand and broader cyclical slowdown, which tends to foreshadow negative equity returns over the next quarter. The signal is most informative when housing starts fall below their recent trend.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls > 8% from its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if housing-start drawdown triggers do not precede negative 60d SPX returns on average, or if the triggered set has positive mean returns for two consecutive rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 growth is positive and RSAFS growth is also positive, the economy is broadening rather than just inflationary, supporting earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The interaction is essential because GDP alone without sales confirmation is weaker.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous GDPC1 and RSAFS growth does not improve 60d SPX returns relative to either series alone, or if the combined-growth regime fails to show positive average returns in rolling 3-year tests.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle labor tightening regime, a still-low UNRATE combined with continued PAYEMS gains supports risk appetite and implies positive S&P 500 returns over the next week. The signal is regime-conditional because it weakens once unemployment starts rising materially.
failure_condition: Reject if low-unemployment plus payroll-growth periods do not produce positive 5d SPX returns more often than not, or if the signal turns negative after controlling for UNRATE trend in rolling walk-forward tests.
